venue: Frost Bank Center, San Antonio, TX

OKC.close_result: OKC scored 110; under all listed TT bands cashed

SAS.close_result: SAS scored 130; over all listed TT bands cashed

closing_result_eval.moneyline: SAS upset ML won at roughly +190 to +207 range
closing_result_eval.spread: SAS +6.5/+7 covered by 26.5 to 27 points; OKC -6.5/-7 failed hard
closing_result_eval.total: Final total 240; over 235.5 cashed by 4.5 points and under 236.5 failed by 3.5 points

line_read_for_model.market_assumption: OKC favored by 6.5 to 7 despite recent SAS upset in Cup semifinal
line_read_for_model.model_postmortem_correction: Market still priced OKC control too heavily and underweighted SAS matchup-specific disruption and second-half creation ceiling.
line_read_for_model.edge_classification: SAS spread and SAS team total were the cleanest pregame edges in hindsight; OKC team total under was structurally supported by rolling-window suppression tags. Full-game over was live but more variance-driven through SAS Q4 efficiency spike.

series_context.status: SAS defeated OKC for second time in 11 days
series_context.prior_meeting: SAS 111, OKC 109 on 2025-12-13 NBA Cup Semifinal
series_context.okc_context: OKC entered as defending champion profile and suffered worst loss of season to date

q1.summary: High-efficiency opening with 5 ties and 3 lead changes. Spurs survived OKC early rim/SGA pressure through Barnes scoring, Vassell rebounding, Harper playmaking, and early Wembanyama bench impact.

q2.summary: OKC briefly regained control behind SGA and Isaiah Joe spacing. Spurs remained inside contact range despite Fox free throw misses and turnover leakage. Halftime: OKC 60, SAS 58.

q3.summary: Game flipped through defensive disruption. OKC committed 6 turnovers for 14 Spurs points. Harper steals, Castle pressure, Barnes/Vassell transition threes, and Keldon Johnson shotmaking created the decisive shift.

q4.summary: Spurs detonated the game late: 15/19 FG, 6/9 from three, 13 assists, only 1 turnover. Wembanyama hit the separation three, Castle followed with another, and San Antonio closed with full offensive control.

Keldon Johnson.note: Primary scoring accelerant; punished OKC bench and transition coverage gaps.

Stephon Castle.note: Late-game shotmaking and defensive pressure helped break the game open.

Victor Wembanyama.note: Block streak ended at 101, but spacing/rim gravity still shifted OKC coverage and supported the fourth-quarter run.

Dylan Harper.note: Low scoring but high-leverage creation/defensive disruption. Season-high 5 steals and 10 assists.

Shai Gilgeous-Alexander.note: SGA scored efficiently, but OKC could not generate enough free throws, transition, or secondary creation.

sas_second_half_surge.note: Spurs outscored OKC by 22 in the second half and created the decisive separation.

okc_turnover_conversion_failure.note: OKC turnovers became high-value Spurs scoring possessions, especially in Q3.

okc_free_throw_suppression.note: OKC scored efficiently from the field but lacked free throw volume to withstand Spurs shotmaking runs.

wemby_geometry_still_active.note: Even without a recorded block, Wembanyama's presence continued to alter lineup geometry, offensive spacing, and OKC defensive coverage.

lineup_rebounding_signals.sas_top_lineup: Fox, Kornet, Vassell, Castle, Barnes: 8:25, 11 rebounds, 4 OREB
lineup_rebounding_signals.okc_top_lineup: Hartenstein, Gilgeous-Alexander, Dort, Holmgren, Williams: 8:25, 11 rebounds, 2 OREB

rolling_window_updates.series_signal: SAS has now produced two distinct win scripts vs OKC in 11 days: close-game Cup survival and full second-half separation blowout.
rolling_window_updates.model_implication: Do not price OKC vs SAS as a standard Thunder dominance matchup. Spurs length, Wembanyama geometry, secondary creators, and bench scoring materially alter OKC's transition and late-game efficiency profile.

spread.market_reference_from_this_game: OKC -6.5/-7 despite recent SAS Cup win
spread.postmortem_adjustment: downgrade OKC cover probability; upgrade SAS plus-points band

total.note: This game went over 235.5 due to extreme SAS Q4 efficiency. Treat totals as bifurcated: OKC offense can be suppressed, but SAS transition/shotmaking can create overs if OKC live-ball turnovers spike.

team_totals.sas_tt: upgrade if line <=115.5 with healthy Wembanyama/Castle/Johnson roles
team_totals.okc_tt: downgrade if priced above 119.5-121.5 against SAS length/halfcourt pressure
